hooks.text: Paying your supplier in US dollars feels like the safe choice. It's also the choice that quietly costs you two percent more on every order.
Every business owner pays Chinese suppliers in US dollars because it feels safe. There's a reason your supplier would rather you didn't, and it saves you money.
Your US dollar payment gets converted twice before it reaches your supplier. Two percent of a twenty thousand dollar order is four hundred dollars, gone, baked into the rate.
A business owner paid his factory in US dollars for two years because it felt safe. The supplier finally admitted he'd been padding every quote to cover it.
Every US dollar payment you send a Chinese supplier loses two percent to a second conversion you never see. On a twenty thousand dollar order, that's four hundred dollars.
